title: Morgan Stanley reiterates Li Auto stock rating on buyback plan
summary: Morgan Stanley has maintained its "Overweight" rating and $26.00 price target for Li Auto (NASDAQ:LI) after the company announced its first share buyback program of up to $1 billion. This move comes as Li Auto's shares have fallen significantly, and the company plans to fund the buyback from its substantial cash reserves. Morgan Stanley views the buyback as a protection against downside risk and sees upcoming model launches and the company's AI pivot as potential catalysts for regaining market confidence.

title: Alphabet Inc. Class A stock slides 1.5% amid U.S.-Iran tensions and soaring AI capex concerns
summary: Alphabet Inc. Class A stock fell over 1.5% due to heightened U.S.-Iran tensions, which are driving up oil prices and increasing concerns about energy costs for AI data centers. Investors are also scrutinizing Alphabet's massive $175-185 billion capital expenditure guidance for 2026, which is nearly double the previous year's, sparking fears of compressed margins despite strong revenue growth in Q4. Analyst sentiment remains largely bullish, with a "Moderate Buy" consensus and price targets suggesting upside, but regulatory risks and intense competition in AI and ad tech persist.

title: Amazon Unleashes Zoox Robotaxis In Austin And Miami To Challenge Waymo Dominance
summary: Amazon's Zoox is expanding its robotaxi service to Austin and Miami, initially for employees and then to the public, aiming to challenge Waymo's stronghold in the autonomous ride-hailing market. Zoox has already provided numerous free rides in Las Vegas and San Francisco and plans to integrate its service with Uber's app in Las Vegas this summer, with a Los Angeles rollout planned for mid-2027. This strategic expansion, including a partnership with Uber, seeks to rapidly scale Zoox's presence and accelerate the adoption of its autonomous vehicles.

title: Apple to host June developer conference online with AI updates
summary: Apple announced it will hold its annual Worldwide Developers Conference (WWDC) online from June 8-12, focusing on AI advancements and new software. This comes as Apple has faced challenges in the AI race, with previous developer conferences featuring more incremental AI developments compared to competitors. The company is also reportedly planning to introduce advertising in its Maps app to boost services revenue.

title: Alphabet Inc. Stock: Shares Slide More Than 1.5% in Early Trading as Tech Sector Faces Geopolitical Pressure
summary: Alphabet Inc. shares dropped over 1.5% in early trading, influenced by the U.S.-Iran conflict and elevated energy costs impacting AI infrastructure spending. Investors are balancing the company's strong AI-driven growth and positive Q4 results against concerns stemming from geopolitical tensions, high capital expenditures for AI, and a "higher-for-longer" interest rate environment. Despite analyst confidence and expanding AI offerings, the stock's sensitivity to energy prices and market caution underscore the uncertainties facing the tech giant.

title: Bank of Montreal Teams With CME Group, Google Cloud on Tokenized Cash Platform
summary: Bank of Montreal (BMO) is collaborating with CME Group and Google Cloud to introduce tokenized cash capabilities for institutional clients. This initiative aims to enable secure, 24/7 movement of funds using tokenized dollars within CME's network on Google Cloud Universal Ledger. The service, expected in the second half of 2026 pending regulatory approval, will allow BMO clients to convert U.S. dollars into tokenized deposits for real-time settlement, extended trading hours, and programmable cash applications, addressing growing momentum in tokenized money solutions.

title: [424B2] BANK OF NOVA SCOTIA Prospectu...
summary: The Bank of Nova Scotia (BNS) is offering $11,702,000 in Contingent Income Auto-Callable Securities due March 23, 2029, linked to Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) Class A common stock. These securities pay a contingent quarterly coupon of $26.40 (10.56% per annum) if GOOGL's closing price is at or above a downside threshold of $195.65. The notes are auto-callable if GOOGL's closing price meets or exceeds the call threshold of $301.00, but investors risk losing all principal if the final share price falls below the downside threshold and the notes are not redeemed early.

title: Akamai Technologies Inc. stock hits new 52-week high on NASDAQ amid cybersecurity surge
summary: Akamai Technologies Inc. (AKAM) shares reached a new 52-week high of $114.95 on NASDAQ, driven by strong investor confidence in its cybersecurity and content delivery solutions. The surge is attributed to growing demand for edge computing amid escalating cyber threats and AI adoption, positioning Akamai as a leader benefiting from rising cybersecurity budgets and partnerships with hyperscalers. Analysts rate AKAM as Outperform, with a consensus price target suggesting modest upside, while its shift towards security and AI integration promises multi-year growth.

title: Bank of Montreal Plans Tokenized Cash Platform
summary: Bank of Montreal (BMO) is set to launch a tokenized cash platform, becoming the first bank to offer CME Group’s solution on Google Cloud Universal Ledger. This initiative aims to provide institutional clients with 24/7 tokenized cash services, enabling them to convert dollars into a tokenized instrument for use with margined products at CME. The goal is to create a secure, bank-anchored framework for moving funds at all times.

title: Bank of Montreal introduces tokenized cash and deposit platform
summary: Bank of Montreal (BMO), in collaboration with CME Group and Google Cloud, is launching a new tokenized cash and deposit platform. This platform will enable institutional clients to use tokenized cash for margined products at CME Group and will offer traditional commercial bank funds in digital form for B2B payments and treasury movements. The new capabilities will allow for 24/7 conversion of U.S. dollars into tokenized deposits and cash, aiming to meet global market needs for continuous operations and collateral movement.

title: BMO Introduces Tokenized Cash and Deposit Platform with CME Group and Google Cloud
summary: BMO, in collaboration with CME Group and Google Cloud, is launching a new tokenized cash and deposit platform. This initiative will enable institutional clients to move value continuously for margin, collateral, and settlement workflows using CME Group's network on Google Cloud Universal Ledger. The platform aims to modernize capital markets efficiency by providing 24/7 tokenized cash and deposits, thereby reducing funding gaps and operational friction.

title: The 2026 Microsoft Deep-Dive: Silicon, Surface, and the Crossover Year of AI PCs
summary: Microsoft is positioned at a crucial point in 2026, leading the AI PC transition with its second-generation Copilot+ ecosystem and custom silicon. The company is leveraging its hardware-software synergy to maintain its significant market capitalization by turning Copilot into an "agent" and deploying custom Maia silicon in data centers. Despite market skepticism and regulatory challenges, Microsoft's strong financial health, enterprise refresh cycle, and strategic AI integrations present robust opportunities for continued growth.

title: Alphabet Inc. $GOOG Shares Sold by MassMutual Private Wealth & Trust FSB
summary: MassMutual Private Wealth & Trust FSB reduced its stake in Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG) by 5.6% in the fourth quarter, selling 13,934 shares. Despite this, Alphabet remains a significant holding for them, valued at $73 million, while insider selling by CEO Sundar Pichai and Director John L. Hennessy also occurred. The company's fundamentals remain strong with positive Q4 earnings, a $3.62 trillion market cap, and widespread "Buy" ratings from analysts, although some neutral and negative sentiments regarding regulatory scrutiny and energy supply for AI were noted.

title: HP unveils NearSense for cross-device file sharing with Google
summary: HP Inc. announced HP NearSense, a new connectivity feature enabling cross-device file sharing and interoperability between HP computers and Android devices. Built on Google’s Device-to-Device-Infrastructure (D2DI), it will launch on select HP AI PCs in Spring 2026, simplifying tasks like meeting room access and file transfers. This initiative comes as HP expands its HP IQ on-device AI layer across its product portfolio, collaborating with Google and Qualcomm.

title: Alphabet Inc. $GOOGL Shares Sold by Baldwin Wealth Partners LLC MA
summary: Baldwin Wealth Partners LLC MA reduced its stake in Alphabet Inc. ($GOOGL) by 1.8% in the fourth quarter, selling 6,437 shares but still making it their largest holding at $108.316 million. Insiders have sold a significant number of shares recently, totaling approximately $118.1 million. Despite insider sales, Alphabet reported strong Q4 earnings, beating estimates, and maintains a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts with a target price of $367.18.

title: Hut 8 (HUT) Surges 11.5% on Upbeat Outlook, Analyst Hikes PT by 157%
summary: Hut 8 Corp. (HUT) saw its shares surge by 11.55% after Arete Research issued a "buy" recommendation and increased its price target by 157% to $136. This optimism stems from Hut 8's 15-year leasing deal with Google for its River Bend data center, which has the potential to generate $17.7 billion in revenues. However, the analyst also noted caution regarding Hut 8's significant Bitcoin exposure.

title: Google And Meta's Fate Hangs In The Balance As Jury Signals Deadlock In Social Media Addiction Trial
summary: A jury in a social media addiction trial against Google and Meta has indicated a deadlock, struggling to reach a verdict regarding one of the defendants. The case centers on claims that platforms like YouTube and Instagram led to a young woman's addiction. A Los Angeles judge has urged continued deliberation, warning of a potential mistrial if no unanimous decision is reached.

title: Palo Alto updates security platform to discover AI agents
summary: Palo Alto Networks has updated its Prisma AIRS security platform and enterprise browser to detect and manage AI agents, models, and connections across IT environments. This update includes vulnerability scanning, red team testing simulation, and, following planned acquisitions, will offer an AI Agent Gateway for runtime and identity security. Additionally, the company announced Next Generation Trust Security (NGTS) for managing digital certificate lifecycles, aimed at addressing the increasing complexity and shorter lifespan of these certificates.

title: Google's AI cut contrails 62% on American Airlines flights. Getting the whole industry to follow could be a harder problem.
summary: A real-world trial by Google and American Airlines demonstrated that AI-powered contrail forecasts reduced contrail formation by 62% on avoidance flights and resulted in a 69% reduction in climatological warming. However, only a small percentage of eligible flights actually utilized the avoidance option, highlighting the challenges of operationalizing such changes across the entire airline industry. Despite the scientific uncertainties and operational hurdles, broader industry momentum and new partnerships suggest a growing interest in using AI for contrail avoidance.

title: RBC Embeds AI at the Core of Banking, Aiming for $1 Billion in Returns
summary: Royal Bank of Canada (RBC) is integrating artificial intelligence into its core operations, from developer workflows to capital markets and enterprise decision-making, with a goal of achieving up to $1 billion in AI-generated enterprise value by 2027. This strategic shift positions RBC as a leader in scaled AI adoption within the financial sector, leveraging tools like RBC Assist for internal workflows and the Aiden platform for capital markets. The bank's commitment is further solidified by a dedicated AI Group reporting to the CEO and significant annual technology investments.

title: Apple will reportedly bring ads to Apple Maps soon
summary: Apple is reportedly planning to introduce ads to Apple Maps as early as this summer, as part of a broader strategy to increase monetization from its services. These ads are expected to be sponsored listings, similar to those found in the App Store, appearing when users search for local businesses. While this move is a standard practice in the navigation industry, user reactions to similar rumors in the past have been negative.

title: BAXTER INTERNATIONAL INC ($BAX) Former Chair of the Board, President and CEO 2025 Pay Revealed
summary: José E. Almeida, the former Chair of the Board, President, and CEO of BAXTER INTERNATIONAL INC ($BAX), is estimated to have received $7,666,044 in compensation for 2025, a significant decrease from 2024. This information comes from a recent DEF14A SEC filing. The article also provides details on congressional stock trading, hedge fund activity, analyst ratings, and price targets for $BAX.

title: Apple plans to put ads in Apple Maps as part of services push
summary: Apple is reportedly planning to integrate ads into Apple Maps as part of its broader push into services. This advertising model will function similarly to Google Maps, allowing brands and retailers to bid for ad placements related to search queries. This move marks another step in Apple's strategy to expand its services revenue.

title: DTE Energy (NYSE: DTB) sets 2026 EPS guidance and $36.5B growth plan
summary: DTE Energy (NYSE: DTB) has released its 2026 earnings per share guidance between $7.59 and $7.73, projecting 6%-8% growth over its 2025 midpoint. The company also announced an expanded five-year capital investment plan of $36.5 billion for 2026-2030, which includes significant investments in DTE Electric, DTE Gas, and DTE Vantage. This growth strategy is heavily influenced by new data center agreements with Oracle and Google, expected to add substantial load and offer affordability benefits to existing customers.

title: Wearable Devices Ltd. (WLDS) registers 33,644 shares from IPO and underwriter warrants
summary: Wearable Devices Ltd. has filed a post-effective amendment to register 33,644 ordinary shares for issuance upon the exercise of outstanding IPO Warrants and Underwriter’s Warrants from its 2022 IPO. The company anticipates receiving up to $15.8 million if all IPO warrants are exercised and $995 thousand from Underwriter's Warrants. These proceeds are earmarked for manufacturing, marketing, R&D, sales support, loan repayment, and executive bonuses, with management retaining discretion over their use.

title: International Business Machines Corp Stock (IBM) Moved Up by 3.04% on Mar 23: Drivers Behind the Movement
summary: International Business Machines Corp (IBM) stock rose by 3.04% due to its strengthened focus on AI and hybrid cloud solutions, strategic acquisitions like Confluent, and robust Q4 2025 financial results. Partnerships with NVIDIA and practical AI applications for the Masters Tournament also contributed to positive market sentiment, despite ongoing legal risks and competitive pressures. The company's strong performance and positive industry trends outweighed some analyst concerns and legal challenges.

title: Google partners with Georgia Power to fund home energy upgrades amid data center surge
summary: Google and Georgia Power have announced a partnership to establish a $5 million fund aimed at boosting energy efficiency for local residents. This initiative will help certain households cover the costs of upgrades, potentially leading to lower power bills. The collaboration comes amidst a surge in data center development, indicating a push towards more sustainable energy practices.

title: CMS vows to ditch faxes for electronic claims attachments
summary: CMS has issued a final rule aimed at eliminating the use of fax machines and snail mail for healthcare claim attachments by mandating electronic standards for secure digital exchange and electronic signatures. This move, which was first proposed in 2022, is expected to save approximately $781 million annually and will require HIPAA-covered entities to comply within two years of its May 26, 2026 effective date. While the rule focuses on claims attachments, it intentionally excluded prior authorizations to avoid conflicts with existing interoperability mandates.

title: Apple Eyes Ads In Maps To Boost Services Revenue: Report
summary: Apple Inc. (AAPL) is reportedly planning to introduce promoted listings within its Maps application, expanding its advertising business to further monetize its services segment. This move, similar to Google Maps' strategy, involves an auction-based system for businesses to bid on keywords for user searches, and is expected to roll out later in 2026. This initiative aims to bolster Apple's services revenue, which already exceeds $100 billion annually, alongside its growing market share in smartphones.

title: JPMorgan last month launched a basket of credit default swaps in Alphabet Inc., Amazon.com, Meta Platforms, Microsoft, and Oracle Corp - Bloomberg News
summary: JPMorgan recently launched a basket of credit default swaps encompassing major tech companies such as Alphabet Inc., Amazon.com, Meta Platforms, Microsoft, and Oracle Corp. This initiative, reported by Bloomberg News, offers investors a new method to hedge against the debt risk of these prominent firms, particularly in the context of the growing AI sector. The article also provides a company profile for Microsoft, detailing its business segments, financials, and recent news regarding its involvement with OpenAI.

title: IonQ Revenue Surge Puts Quantum Stocks Back in Focus, but QTUM ETF Shows Why Hype May Be Ahead
summary: Quantum computing stocks, including IonQ, Rigetti, and D-Wave, saw gains, driven by IonQ's significant revenue surge to $130 million in 2025. Despite this, the Defiance Quantum ETF (QTUM) with $3.5 billion in assets has limited exposure (2.92%) to pure-play quantum stocks, instead focusing on a broader range of tech companies. This highlights a disconnect where public markets bet on future potential over current profits, with government backing also supporting the nascent sector.

title: 2026 Outlook: Revisiting Our Tech Predictions
summary: FTI Consulting's S&T Technology Practice reviews its 2025 AI predictions, noting successes in Generative AI and synthetic data, and areas still developing like XR and quantum computing. For 2026, the firm forecasts a shift towards the economics and integration of AI, highlighting challenges in power, memory, and geopolitical factors, with a focus on value creation from integrated, industry-specific AI solutions.

title: Alphabet Stock Forecast: Can GOOGL Deliver Nearly 40% Gains in 12 Months?
summary: Alphabet (GOOGL) stock has seen a recent cool-off despite a strong rally driven by the launch of Gemini 3 and significant revenue from its Tensor Processing Units (TPUs). Geopolitical tensions and concerns over rising capital expenditures, projected to reach $175 billion to $185 billion in 2026, have contributed to this stalled momentum. However, analysts remain optimistic, with some forecasting a nearly 40% upside in the next 12 months, contingent on sustained revenue growth and improved margins from AI investments.

title: Apple may bring advertising to Apple Maps app soon, report says; WWDC dates confirmed (AAPL:NASDAQ)
summary: Apple is reportedly planning to integrate advertising into its Apple Maps application, with an announcement potentially coming as early as this month. This move could expand the company's advertising ecosystem. The article also confirms the dates for Apple's annual Worldwide Developers Conference (WWDC).

title: Justices Reject Case Alleging Google-Apple Search Pact
summary: The U.S. Supreme Court has declined to review lower court rulings that dismissed a lawsuit contending Google engaged in anticompetitive behavior by paying Apple not to develop its own search engine. This decision upholds the dismissal of claims made against the tech giants regarding their search pact. The case had previously been rejected by a California federal judge and the Ninth Circuit.

title: Rosenblatt has a stark message for investors in Google-backed stock
summary: A five-star Rosenblatt analyst, Chris Brendler, has reiterated a Buy rating on TeraWulf Inc. (WULF) with a $23 price target, implying over 52% upside. Google recently increased its stake in TeraWULF to 14% and committed up to $3.2 billion in funding. Despite considerable debt and current unprofitability, TeraWulf's use of nuclear and hydroelectric power for its bitcoin mining operations gives it a structural cost advantage and strong ESG compliance in the sector, leading to a "Strong Buy" consensus among analysts.

title: Quantum Computing Meets Wall Street: Running Real Portfolio Optimization on Trapped-Ion Hardware
summary: IonQ successfully demonstrated real portfolio optimization on trapped-ion quantum hardware, showing that increasing qubit count from 36 to 64 systematically improves portfolio quality for 250 S&P 500 assets. The study, co-authored with Kipu Quantum, used a hybrid quantum-classical pipeline to address the NP-hard cardinality-constrained portfolio selection problem. This research highlights the significant potential of increasing qubit availability and fidelity in quantum processors to solve complex financial optimization challenges that are currently intractable for classical methods.

title: NetApp in the age of AI: balancing sovereignty and cloud
summary: NetApp is addressing the complex issue of data sovereignty, particularly in Europe, by balancing regulatory requirements with the need for advanced AI capabilities and cloud partnerships. The company's approach involves working with major cloud providers on sovereign cloud initiatives and developing on-premises AI infrastructure solutions like AFX and the AI Data Engine. NetApp emphasizes customer data ownership and aims to optimize storage and AI data preparation, offering a proven platform to navigate the evolving landscape of AI and cloud computing.

title: Alphabet Stock (GOOG) Opinions on Earnings Release
summary: Alphabet's (GOOG) recent earnings beat expectations, driven by AI-powered cost reductions and a strong Google Cloud backlog, leading to mixed opinions among traders and analysts. While some see potential for a breakout, others are cautious due to broader market headwinds. The company's AI leadership and strong fundamentals continue to fuel long-term investment bets despite near-term volatility.

title: Triple Flag Precious Metals Corp share price
summary: This article provides details on the current share price, key financial metrics, earnings, and historical performance of Triple Flag Precious Metals Corp (TFPM). As of March 23, 2026, the stock price is $31.65, with a market capitalization of $6.34 billion and significant returns over the past few years. TFPM operates as a precious metals streaming and royalty company, offering capital solutions to the mining industry.

title: SoundHound AI Peet’s Rollout Tests Retail Adoption And Execution Risk
summary: SoundHound AI (SOUN) is expanding its voice-powered Employee Assist agent, branded "BaristAI", across all Peet's Coffee stores nationwide, moving beyond pilot programs. This rollout tests the company's ability to scale its conversational AI technology in real-world retail settings and provides a concrete reference for future partnerships. Despite leadership changes and unprofitability forecasts, the deployment offers a crucial proof point for SoundHound AI's growth strategy and its agentic, edge-focused AI approach.

title: Elliott Investment Management takes multibillion-dollar stake in Synopsys, plans engagement
summary: Elliott Investment Management has reportedly acquired a multibillion-dollar stake in Synopsys Inc., a chip-design software company. The activist investor plans to engage with Synopsys to enhance how it monetizes its software and services portfolio. This move is expected to push Synopsys towards extracting greater value from its offerings, which are crucial for the semiconductor and technology industries, as Elliott is known for advocating for strategic changes to boost shareholder returns.

title: Bernstein Upgrades CVS Health (CVS) Stock to Outperform from Market Perform
summary: Bernstein analyst Lance Wilkes upgraded CVS Health (CVS) stock to "Outperform" from "Market Perform," raising the price target to $94 from $91 due to positive Medicare Advantage outlook and stable earnings in pharmacy businesses. This upgrade follows the PBM bill passage and the FTC settlement with Cigna, clearing uncertainties for the company. Additionally, CVS Health has partnered with Google Cloud to enhance healthcare experiences and launched Health100, an AI-powered health technology services subsidiary, highlighting its strategic moves in the evolving healthcare landscape.

title: ETFs Investing in Alphabet Inc. Class A Stocks
summary: This article lists various ETFs that include Alphabet Inc. Class A (GOOGL) stocks in their portfolios. It provides detailed financial information for each ETF, such as market value, weight of GOOGL stock, issuer, management style, expense ratio, AUM, price, and 3-year total return. The ETFs cover a range of investment styles, from passive total market and large cap to active communication services and ESG-focused funds.

title: Yeomans Consulting Group Inc. Raises Stock Position in Alphabet Inc. $GOOGL
summary: Yeomans Consulting Group Inc. significantly increased its stake in Alphabet (GOOGL) during Q4, boosting its holdings by 88.2% to 5,548 shares valued at $1.737 million. This comes despite recent insider selling by CEO Sundar Pichai and Director John Hennessy, totaling over $10.7 million. Analysts largely maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating, with an average target price of $367.18, following Alphabet's strong quarterly earnings beat and positive momentum in AI initiatives like "Stitch."

title: Gartner: AI to Power Half of All Cyber Incident Response by 2028
summary: Gartner predicts that by 2028, 50% of all enterprise cybersecurity incident response will be dedicated to custom-built AI applications, largely due to their complexity and current lack of clear incident resolution processes. To mitigate risks, Gartner advises security leaders to engage early in AI application projects, plan resources, and implement adequate security controls. The report also highlights the increasing adoption of AI security platforms, the critical need for AI compliance to avoid hefty fines, and the importance of addressing "AI data debt" for secure AI adoption.

title: OpenAI takes over a 450,000-square-foot Silicon Valley campus
summary: KKR Real Estate Finance Trust Inc. (KREF) and TMG Partners have leased their 450,000-square-foot Class A campus at 350-380 Ellis in Mountain View, California, to OpenAI. The campus, located in Silicon Valley, underwent a significant repositioning and modernization effort led by TMG to create a future-ready workplace. This full-building lease validates the vision of creating a premier headquarters environment for leading technology companies.

title: Alphabet (GOOGL) Stock Drops 13% From All-Time High — Time to Buy the Dip?
summary: Alphabet (GOOGL) stock has fallen 13% from its all-time high, trading below $300 amidst a global market sell-off driven by Middle East tensions. Despite strong Q4 2025 earnings and revenue exceeding $400 billion for the first time, investor sentiment is impacted by the company's substantial 2026 capital expenditure plans for AI infrastructure. Analysts maintain a "Strong Buy" consensus with an average 12-month price target of $351.82, suggesting potential for significant upside.

title: The Backbone of the Billion-Dollar Brain: Broadcom Solidifies AI Infrastructure Dominance After Landmark March Earnings
summary: Broadcom Inc. has solidified its position as a key architect of AI infrastructure, with 44% of its revenue now AI-related, following a stellar March 2026 earnings report. The company announced a landmark partnership with OpenAI to co-develop a custom inference engine, signaling a significant shift towards specialized silicon and away from an Nvidia-only dominance in AI hardware. This move underscores the growing importance of high-speed Ethernet networking, where Broadcom leads, for massive AI deployments and future inference-centric workloads.

title: Building Through the Cycle: A Deep-Dive Analysis of KB Home (NYSE: KBH) in 2026
summary: This article provides a comprehensive analysis of KB Home (NYSE: KBH) in 2026, detailing its historical background, unique "Built-to-Order" business model, and financial resilience. It highlights the company's leadership transition, competitive landscape, and strategic focus on sustainability and first-time buyers amidst a high-interest-rate environment. The piece also discusses the risks and opportunities facing KB Home, including interest rate sensitivity and the millennial/Gen Z housing demand, concluding with an outlook on its future performance.
